# Q-block Convention

**Scope:** Normative  
**Applies to:** Every Q-block generated by `aib-analyze.md` and written to `.aib_memory/input.md ## Questions`.

---

## 1. Purpose

This convention defines the authoritative format templates and format-level rules for Q-blocks — structured questions generated by the analysis workflow when a decision point is tagged `ask`. It is the single source of truth for Q-block structure and MUST be referenced by `aib-analyze.md` for format specification.

---

## 2. Scope & Normative Language

This convention applies to:

- All Q-blocks generated by `aib-analyze.md` and written to `.aib_memory/input.md`.
- Both supported Q-block formats: multiple-choice and free-text.

Out of scope:
- Format selection guidance (which format to use) — defined in `aib-analyze.md`.
- Q-block generation triggers, classification rules, and the Answer Application Sub-flow — defined in `aib-analyze.md`.

Normative keywords **MUST**, **MUST NOT**, **SHALL**, **SHOULD**, and **MAY** are interpreted per BCP 14 (RFC 2119 / RFC 8174).

---

## 3. QID Numbering Rules

- Each Q-block MUST be assigned a stable QID in the format `Q<nnn>` (three zero-padded digits), for example `Q001`, `Q002`.
- QIDs MUST start from `Q001` and increment sequentially.
- If questions already exist in `input.md ## Questions`, the new QIDs MUST continue from the next available number.
- QIDs MUST NOT be reused or reassigned once written.

---

## 4. Multiple-Choice Q-block Format

### 4.1 Template

```
**Q<nnn>**: <question text>
> **Why this matters:** <one-sentence explanation of impact on implementation>
- [ ] Option A: <text> *(recommended)*
- [ ] Option B: <text>
- [ ] Option C: <text>
- [ ] Other: ___
```

### 4.2 Format-Level Rules

- MUST include the `> **Why this matters:**` line immediately after the question text and before the option list.
- MUST provide a minimum of two options (Option A, Option B) with preference of at three and more (Option A, Option B, Option C, ...)
- MUST include an `Other: ___` option.
- MUST mark exactly one option per Q-block as `*(recommended)*`. The recommended option MUST be placed first in the list. All other options MUST NOT carry the marker.
- Each option line MUST begin with `- [ ] ` (dash space bracket-space-bracket space).

---

## 5. Free-Text Q-block Format

### 5.1 Template

```
**Q<nnn>**: <question text>
> **Why this matters:** <one-sentence explanation of impact on implementation>
- Answer: ___
```

### 5.2 Format-Level Rules

- MUST include the `> **Why this matters:**` line immediately after the question text.
- The answer line MUST be exactly `- Answer: ___`.
- The question text MUST explain what information is needed, why it is needed, and the impact of the answer on implementation.
